sutta: monks there are these 7 powers what 7 the power of faith sunda diligence or energy conscience or conscientiousness remorse or compunction mindfulness concentration and wisdom and what monks is the power of faith consider monks the aryan disciple who has faith he believes thus of the tata gathers awakening or enlightenment of the truth he is the exalted 1 arahan fully enlightened repeat with knowledge and practice well gone 1 world no incomparable tamer of tameable men teacher of devas and men buddha exalted 1 this monks is called the power of a and what is the power of diligence or energy consider the aran disciple who abides stirred in energy to be rid of unwholesome states to take to wholesome states he steadfast firm in advance nor lays aside the burden of wholesome states this is called the power of diligence and what is the power of conscience or conscientiousness consider the aryan disciple who is scrupulous he feels conscientious cruple when acting wrongly indeed word and thought on entering into evil and unrighteous ways this month is called the power of conscience or conscientiousness and what is the power of remorse or compunction herein the aryan disciple is remorseful he feels remorseful for having acted wrongly indeed word and thought having of having fallen into evil and right unrighteous ways this monks is called the power of remorse or compunction and what is the power of mindfulness consider the aryan disciple who is mindful possessing a mastery of mindfulness in high degree he remembers and calls to mind things done and said long ago this month is called the power of mindfulness and what is the power concentration considered the aaron disciple who aloo from sense desires enters and abides in the first jhana the second third fourth jana this mountains is called the power of concentration and what's the power of wisdom consider the aryan disciple who is wise he is wise as to the nature of arising and ceasing possessing aryan penetration of the way to the utter destruction of dukkha this month is called the power of wisdom verily amongst these are the 7 powers that's the end of the suta
commentary: here there are 7 powers mentioned it's a little similar to the chapter of the fives we had 5 vectors in the 5 powers now but here they have added another 2 which is conscience and remorse conscience that means sort of a conscientious group when acting wrongly that means this is a is a scrupulous about doing anything wrong and then remorse or compunction or tapa is the ability to be remorseful when you do something wrong there's some people there after they do something wrong they have no remorse that means they have no uttapa and then satya mindfulness here as in other sutas is defined as the ability to remember and call to mind things said and done long ago so satya is when we translate it as mindfulness that means it is more like the ability to remember the means uh to remember to put our attention on body and mind and not and on the 5 external sense objects of sight sounds smells taste and touch and always to remember that body and mind is not i not mine not myself and if we practice sati then we tend to see that this body and this mind is not i not mind myself and we remain equanimous not to be moved by different stages of mind the power of concentration here is defined as the 4 janas and wisdom here is said that the person is wise as to the way of arising and seizing that means the person knows now that all things in the world arise and cease and due to conditions the next few times
